creator: darin@technotic.ca
text:
Hello!

I have decided to get my feet wet by contributing to the ports then documentation for FreeBSD, since I love this community SO much!

I wish to upgrade the current port opencv from the current 2.4.13.1 to  3.x but have come across a conflict between the port and pkg name:

root@seedbox:/usr/ports/graphics/opencv # pkg search opencv | egrep -v "java|core|cvs|plug|py"
opencv2-2.4.13.1_6             Open Source Computer Vision library

root@seedbox:/usr/ports/graphics/opencv # find ../* -name opencv
../opencv
root@seedbox:/usr/ports/graphics/opencv # grep -r 2.4 .
./distinfo:SHA256 (opencv-opencv-2.4.13.1_GH0.tar.gz) = 0d5ce5e0973e3a745f927d1ee097aaf909aae59f787be6d27a03d639e2d96bd7
./distinfo:SIZE (opencv-opencv-2.4.13.1_GH0.tar.gz) = 91773677
./Makefile:PORTVERSION= 2.4.13.1
./files/patch-cmake__OpenCVFindLibsGUI.cmake:+++ ./cmake/OpenCVFindLibsGUI.cmake        2013-12-10 09:22:40.000000000 -0500
./files/patch-cmake__OpenCVVersion.cmake:--- ./cmake/OpenCVVersion.cmake.orig   2013-10-24 00:12:48.000000000 -0400

Should I upgrade the opencv port and request the pkg name be changed later on to prevent conflicts or work on the opencv2 port in order to align with the current pkg name?

Cheers,
